content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable David Roberti 
state Senator 
State Capitol 
Sacramento, 
CA 
95814 
Dear Senator Roberti: 
March 5, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
FPPC No. A-86-062 
This is in response to your letter regarding a notice you 
received from the Los Angeles Turf Club that they had reported you 
as the recipient of a dinner on their Report of Lobbyist Employer 
(Form 650). 
You indicated that you did not attend the dinner. 
Since you did not accept the invitation, you should notify 
Mr. Engle of the Los Angeles Turf Club to amend the Club's Form 
650 to delete you as the recipient of the gift. 
Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have 
any additional questions. 
Sincerely, 
/ 
.. , 
A 
\........ /: 
{I.ti.a j:!t:ut/'...£<1../ 
,J 
Alice Hughes 
Technical Assistance & Analysis 
AH:kt 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


STATE 0 F CALI FO RN IA 
DAVID ROBERTI 
PRESIDENT PRO TEMPORE 
February 18, 1986 
Ms. Alice Hughes 
Documents Examiner 
SENATE 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street, 8th Floor 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Hughes: 
Subject: 
Schedule F, (Gifts) for Form 721 
to be submitted March 3, 1986 
A discrepancy may exist In the Schedule F (Gifts) section of 
the above report. 
A Los Angeles Turf Club, Arcadia, California letter (copy enclosed) 
stated a dinner event was reported under date of April 29, 1985 
in the amount of $105.50; this represented a dinner at Lautrec 
Restaurant, 2711 Fulton Avenue, Sacramento, CA. 
I believe this statement is in error as I did not attend this dinner. 
The mistake is an honest error since I was invited to attend the 
dinner but did not accept the invitation. 
If there is any problem, please inform me. 
Thank you. 
Sincerely, 
DAVID ROBERTI 
DR/mbg 
Encl: 
L.A. Turf Club 1 tr 
dted Sep. 4, 1985

sections.question: You indicated that you did not attend the dinner. Since you did not accept the invitation, you should notify Mr. Engle of the Los Angeles Turf Club to amend the Club's Form 650 to delete you as the recipient of the gift.
sections.conclusion: Since you did not accept the invitation, you should notify Mr. Engle of the Los Angeles Turf Club to amend the Club's Form 650 to delete you as the recipient of the gift.
sections.question_synthetic: Senator Roberti reported receiving a dinner gift from the Los Angeles Turf Club on a Form 721 Schedule F, but clarified he did not actually attend the dinner event and had declined the invitation. What action should be taken regarding this discrepancy?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises Senator Roberti to contact the Los Angeles Turf Club and request that they amend their Form 650 report to remove him as a gift recipient, since he did not attend the dinner.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (Feb 18, 1986) and FPPC response letter (Mar 5, 1986). Question and conclusion sections not formally labeled with headers but clearly present in the response. Extracted question text from FPPC response letter as this is the formal advice letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You indicated that you did not attend the dinner. Since you did not accept the invitation, you should notify Mr. Engle of the Los Angeles Turf Club to amend the Club's Form 650 to delete you as the recipient of the gift.

CONCLUSION: Since you did not accept the invitation, you should notify Mr. Engle of the Los Angeles Turf Club to amend the Club's Form 650 to delete you as the recipient of the gift.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Senator David Roberti's inquiry about a reported dinner gift he did not accept, advising him to request amendment of the lobbyist employer's gift report form.
